task_completion.description: The Markups module is accessed via the menu or toolbar.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Markups' module panel is visible in the sidebar, or the Markups toolbar icons are active/highlighted.

task_completion.description: A plane markup object has been created in the scene.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A plane geometry is visible in the 3D view, or a new entry (default name 'P') appears in the Markups list.

task_completion.description: The plane markup is renamed to include 'Midsagittal'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Name column in the Markups list shows a name containing 'Midsagittal' (e.g., 'Midsagittal_Plane').

task_completion.description: The plane is oriented sagittally (vertical, separating left/right).
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 3D view, the plane appears vertical relative to the head orientation, effectively separating the left and right hemispheres.

task_completion.description: The plane is positioned at the anatomical midline.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The plane passes through the center of the brain volume, aligning with the interhemispheric fissure and corpus callosum.

task_completion.description: The agent saves a screenshot of the result.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save Screenshot' dialog is visible, the Screen Capture module is active, or a file browser shows 'midsagittal_plane.png'.

integrity.description: The agent is using the actual 3D Slicer GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the standard 3D Slicer interface with 3D/slice views and module panels.

integrity.description: The plane is a rendered 3D object, not an annotation drawn on an image.
integrity.visual_evidence: The plane interacts with the 3D volume (e.g., transparency, intersection) and is not a flat 2D drawing overlay.

integrity.description: The agent performed the steps interactively.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show intermediate states (e.g., module selection, renaming) rather than the final result appearing instantly via a single terminal command.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The MRHead dataset uses RAS coordinates. The midsagittal plane is defined by a normal vector of [1.0, 0.0, 0.0] (Sagittal) and is centered at X=0 (Left-Right center). The plane must appear vertical in the 3D view, dividing the brain symmetrically.
